rulings.Case Title: ADRIATIC INSURANCE COMPANY VS. MOURAD HAMMA HANNACHI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Vacate The Order Granting Df'S Mtn To Vacate The Order Setting Aside The Dfault Of Issa Dughman & App For Renewal Of Mtn To Set Aside The Default Judgment Of Same
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 8, 2008 LINE 4. CROSS-DEFENDANT ISSA FUAD DUGHMAN'S MOTION To Vacate The Order Granting DEFENDANT'S MOTION To Vacate The Order Setting Aside The DEfault Of Issa Dughman & Application For Renewal Of motion To Set Aside The Default Judgment Of Same: THE RENEWED MOTION OF CROSS-DEFENDANT ISSA FUAD DUGHMAN TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT OF SEPTEMBER 1, 2006 IS DENIED. THE FOLLOWING UNDISPUTED FACTS CONFIRM THAT DUGHMAN'S PLACE OF RESIDENCE WAS 172 EDGEMONT, DALY CITY WHEN HE WAS SERVED WITH THE COMPLAINT, EITHER PERSONALLY OR BY LEAVING THE COMPLAINT WITH DUGHMAN'S FATHER: THE POLICE REPORT, THE INTERROGATORY ANSWERS IN THE RELATED CASE AND MR. DUGHMAN'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD ENNEY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 8, 2008 LINE 5. DEFENDANT IMO INDUSTRIES INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. THE MCCLAREN DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT ON THE ISSUE OF EXPOSURE. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD ENNEY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 8, 2008 LINE 5. DEFENDANT WARREN PUMPS, L.L.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON THE FACTUALLY DEVOID INTERROGATORY RESPONSES: DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DYDEK AND MCCLAREN DECLARATIONS ARE OVERRULED. THE COURT CONSIDERS DEFENDANT'S SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY BASED ON DEFENDANTS FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY FOLLOWED BY THE MCCLAREN DECLARATION FILED IN OPPOSITION. BASED ON SAN DIEGO WATERCRAFT AND VILLA, THE COURT CONSIDERS ALL OF THE EVIDENCE BEFORE IT AND CONCLUDES THAT PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT WOULD PROVIDE A BASIS FOR A TRIER OF FACT TO CONCLUDE THAT DECEDENT WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS PLACED ON THE PUMP BY DEFENDANT, I.E. PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH EXPOSURE TO ORIGINAL EQUIPMENT. THE COURT PREVIOUSLY HAS REJECTED THE DUTY TO WARN AS TO REPLACEMENT PARTS OF OTHERS. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCISCO MELVIN QUIRARTE AS TRUSTEE OF THE VS. CENTRAL GARDENS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration;Petition To Compel
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 8, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANT CENTRAL GARDENS, INC.'S Petition To Compel Arbitration: HEARING REQUIRED. IT APPEARS UNDER FITZHUGH THAT THE FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION MAY NOT BE SUBJECT TO ARBITRATION. IT ALSO APPEARS THAT THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION EMBRACE THE SAME CONDUCT. HOWEVER, CAUSES OF ACTION THREE, FOUR AND SEVEN RELATE TO OTHER SUBJECTS. THERE IS AN ARBITRATION CLAUSE THAT EMBRACES ALL OF THE CLAIMS AND FITZHUGH RAISES THE QUESTION AT 476 OF THE EFFECT OF "TOSS[ING}] IN A CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGING VIOLATIONS OF THE PATIENTS BILL OF RIGHTS". FITZHUGH MAY WELL BE DISTINGUISHED GIVEN THE DISTINCT CLAIMS OF INDIVIDUALS WHO WERE NOT PARTIES TO THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT. THE PARTIES ARE TO ADDRESS THESE ISSUES. =(302/PJM/AY
